input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-98da31864d7d43b98929d34ca748541f/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.content: The MSBuild property didn't work. The correct approach for Microsoft.Testing.Platform is the `--minimum-expected-tests` option. Let me fix this:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The property `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` doesn't seem to be working. Let me check the correct property name. 

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the Microsoft.Testing.Platform uses `--ignore-exit-code` or the MSBuild property might be different. Let me check the correct approach.

The correct MSBuild property for Microsoft.Testing.Platform to handle zero tests is actually different. Let me think...

For MSTest with Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner, the property to control this behavior might be:
- `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` - but this might not exist
- There might be a command-line option `--ignore-exit-code 8`


- Or a runsettings configuration

Looking at the Microsoft.Testing.Platform documentation, I should use `--minimum-expected-tests 0` as a command-line option or set it via `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` in the project file. The exit code 8 specifically means "no tests found", so I need to configure the platform to either ignore that condition or set the minimum expected tests to zero.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: That didn't work either. Let me check the log file and also try passing it directly on the command line.

data.content: The log reveals `--minimum-expected-tests` doesn't accept 0, but there's an `--ignore-exit-code` option. Exit code 8 means "no tests found". Let me use that instead:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The log shows that `--minimum-expected-tests` requires a "non-zero positive integer". So setting it to 0 is not valid. But I can see there's an `--ignore-exit-code` option that takes specific exit codes to ignore:

```
--ignore-exit-code
    Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes
    (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)
```

So the fix is to use `--ignore-exit-code 8` to ignore exit code 8 (which is the "no tests found" exit code).

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (matches VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**Fixed.** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when zero tests are discovered (unlike VSTest which returned 0). The fix is adding this to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

This tells the test runner to map exit code 8 ("no tests found") back to 0, restoring the VSTest behavior. Actual test failures will still produce a non-zero exit code.
